page: OVHcloud b3-640 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 160 dedicated physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. The system is equipped with 640 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark testing, the b3-640 demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is also strong, with read-only small block memory bandwidth reaching the top 10% tier. For specialized tasks, the instance achieves top-tier results in software compilation with Clang and ray tracing. Redis SET operations also perform in the top 10% tier, registering 170.81 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the b3-640 offers high compute density with dedicated physical cores, making it highly efficient for parallel processing workloads. However, there are notable tradeoffs. The local NVMe storage capacity is limited to 400 GB, which is relatively small given the massive 160-core compute capacity. Additionally, while prompt processing for small and medium language models is strong, text generation for large 70B models falls into the bottom 10% due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

This instance is configured for highly parallelized, CPU-bound workloads. It is suitable for software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, high-throughput Redis databases, static web serving, and CPU-based prompt processing for smaller machine learning models.
description: OVHcloud b3-640 is a general-purpose server featuring 160 dedicated physical AMD EPYC cores running at 2.3 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. With hyperthreading disabled, it provides 640 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core), a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong memory bandwidth. However, the 400 GB local storage is modest for a server of this scale, and text generation for large language models is slow due to the absence of a GPU. This instance is optimized for highly parallelized CPU tasks, software compilation, and high-throughput database operations.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-640 is a general-purpose server featuring 160 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 640 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A dedicated 160-core x86_64 platform with balanced memory and 20 Gbps network bandwidth for parallelized workloads.
